properties.remark: An ice jam on the Grand River is causing minor flooding at Grand River Landing Metropark. The park has closed as a result. No other flooding impacts have been observed.
Law enforcement observed a tornado at OH-37 and Township Road 168.
Law enforcement observed a tornado looking north from OH-37 and Township Road 168.

Based on several social media photos and videos, eyewitness reports, and law enforcement reports, a brief and intermittent tornado occurred from the extreme eastern part of Hancock County to the western part of Seneca County. Survey team was unable to access fields to observe any damage. Tornado remained over farmland and did not impact any structures.
Utility pole down and hit a vehicle.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 3 SW Jenera. Emergency management reported several structures with significant damage, including roofs blown off of homes and partial collapse of barns along Township Road 29. Several large trees down as well. Report corrected for time based on radar.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 3 WSW Jenera. Report of damage to houses near Township Road 29 and Township Road 12. Report corrected for time based on radar.

An EF-1 tornado with maximum estimated winds of 100 MPH developed in Orange Township in southwestern Hancock County. The tornado tracked 3.3 miles towards the east, lifting in Van Buren Township. The tornado damaged 5 homes and damaged or destroyed several farm buildings. The maximum width was about 100 yards.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Corrects previous hail report from Bloomville. Two to two and a half inch size hail. Report corrected for time based on radar.
Corrects previous hail report from Bloomville. Two and a half to three inch size hail. Report corrected for time based on radar.

Tornado began along Marsh Road just northeast of New Washington in Crawford County and continued east into northern Richland County, lifting along Willet Road east of Plymouth Township. The peak intensity was EF-2 with winds estimated at 120 MPH in Auburn Township and Plymouth Township. The max width was 250 yards. The tornado was on the ground for slightly over 10 miles.
Report of damage to houses near Township Road 29 and Township Road 12.

A damage survey conducted by county emergency management in coordination with the National Weather Service confirms a weak EF0 tornado that touched down near an industrial building parking lot before moving into a golf course and producing tree damage and dislodging stone tiles from a footbridge. The tornado had estimated maximum wind speeds of 65 mph, a path length of 0.31 miles, and an estimated path width of 10 yards.
